danger_signals.visual_form: CPU/player 2 character animation shift to attack pose
danger_signals.meaning: incoming attack threat; player must defend or dodge

danger_signals.visual_form: digitized voice announces 'Half point' or 'Full point' in Japanese
danger_signals.meaning: successful opponent strike landed; round advantage lost
clear adversarial relationship; visual distinction of player vs enemy

safety_signals.visual_form: opponent knockback/stagger animation; point awarded visually
safety_signals.meaning: player has secured advantage; threat momentarily neutralized

safety_signals.visual_form: scene change to avoid-obstacle minigame (potted plants, boards, bull)
safety_signals.meaning: temporary reprieve from direct combat; opportunity to gain safe points

safety_signals.visual_form: player character (white gi) standing while opponent (red gi) is down
safety_signals.meaning: player in winning position; temporary safety from counter-attack

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: positioned on left side of screen in default layout
positioned on right side of screen; multiple opponent characters across levels (traveling tournament format)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: two karateka facing each other on tournament location background; dojo at level 1, various international venues (harbor, garden, desert, city) in subsequent levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: both players can input attacks; judge announces scores; background remains static

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent recoils/staggers; point awarded on screen; judge announces point value
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player gains 1 point (full) or 0.5 points (half); opponent cannot immediately counter; transition to next round if match not won

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player character recoils; opponent gains point; judge announces opponent's point
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: game progresses to next match if best-of-three not lost; if match lost, game ends

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: background changes to isolated arena; obstacle visualization (potted plants flying, wooden boards, charging bull sprite)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must execute evasion/striking mechanics against environmental hazards rather than opponent; successful completion awards bonus points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: background scene transitions to new international location (12 different venues in Player vs Player version); opponent character may change or repeat
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: difficulty increases subtly; same mechanical ruleset applies across all scenes; narrative framing shifts from dojo training to worldwide tournament competition

environmental_storytelling.example: 12 distinct tournament venues (dojo, harbor, Mount Fuji garden, bamboo forest, desert with tipis, Holland windmills, city, Japanese garden, forest path at night) establish a global martial arts competition narrative without explicit dialogue
white vs red gi creates instant visual storytelling of opposition and tournament bracket convention (common in martial arts media) without requiring text explanation
day scenes (harbor, gardens, city) shift to night scenes (final forest path) suggesting temporal passage through tournament; environmental mood reinforces competition stakes
evasion challenge with potted plants, board-breaking, and bull-stopping reflect traditional martial arts demonstration tropes, reinforcing karate cultural context without cutscenes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: stands in neutral karate stance facing opponent; awaits input
executes one of 24 moves via dual-joystick input; animation plays until move completes or is interrupted
recoils/staggers backward; brief stun window before recovery; opponent gains point
mirrors neutral stance; awaits player input or executes AI-determined attack pattern
executes attack animation at intervals based on difficulty level; no health bar system means hit = point awarded immediately
recoils; loses positioning advantage; player gains point; recovers to neutral stance
provides digitized speech announcements ('Half point', 'Full point') to confirm successful strikes; audio-visual confirmation of world-state change

notes: Karate Champ uses minimal UI and symbolic communication. The absence of a health bar creates a unique risk-reward dynamic: every landed hit is immediately decisive, making visual opponent positioning and animation cues critical for threat assessment. The game communicates world-state primarily through character animation, background transitions, judge announcements, and point visualization. The white vs. red gi distinction is the core faction/opposition signal. Environmental storytelling through venue changes (dojo → international tournament locations) frames progression narrative. Bonus round alternation between combat and obstacle challenges creates pacing variety and visual world-state shifts.
